title: Astronauts Fly to the Moon on Artemis II Mission
summary: Four astronauts are on a big trip to the Moon in a spacecraft called Orion. They left Earth's orbit after their engine fired for almost six minutes. This engine burn, called translunar injection, worked perfectly. The astronauts are feeling good and excited. They are going around the far side of the Moon and back, which is the first time humans have gone beyond Earth's orbit since 1972. From space, Earth looks smaller as they move away. The crew thanked everyone who helped make this mission happen. They will travel farther from Earth than anyone before, maybe breaking a record. Even after the engine burn, they can still turn back to Earth if needed. This mission shows what people can achieve together.

keywords.explanation: a big engine burn to go to the Moon
the path something takes around a planet or moon

questions.question: How many astronauts are on the Artemis II mission?
What is the name of the spacecraft in the mission?
How long did the engine burn last?
When was the last time humans traveled outside Earth's orbit before this mission?
What does the crew see getting smaller as they move into space?
Can the spacecraft turn back to Earth after the engine burn?

background_read: Artemis II is a NASA mission to send astronauts around the Moon. It is part of a bigger plan to explore space and maybe go to Mars someday. The mission uses the Orion spacecraft, which is designed for deep space travel. Astronauts from different countries work together on this trip. This helps people learn more about space and inspires others to dream big.
Article_Structure: WHO: Four astronauts on the Artemis II mission, including Jeremy Hansen and Reid Wiseman.
WHAT: They left Earth's orbit and are traveling to the Moon in the Orion spacecraft.
WHERE: They are in space, going around the far side of the Moon.
WHY: To explore space, break records, and show what humanity can achieve.

perspectives.description: The mission helps humans learn more about space and the Moon.
